entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop Cicer arietinum Linn. being studied for pest susceptibility

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The lepidopteran pest whose larvae feed on chickpea

entities.name: NO-CHOICE CAGED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Experimental setup where larvae are restricted to a single genotype

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE
entities.description: Controlled environment where chickpea genotypes were grown

entities.name: FEEDING PREFERENCE
entities.description: The choice or tendency of larvae to feed on specific leaf types or genotypes

entities.name: DAMAGE RATING
entities.description: Metric used to quantify the level of feeding damage on the plant

entities.name: LARVAL SURVIVAL
entities.description: The percentage of larvae that survive on a given genotype

entities.name: LARVAL GROWTH
entities.description: The rate of development and weight gain of the larvae

entities.name: ANTIBIOSIS
entities.description: A mechanism of plant resistance that adversely affects the life cycle of the pest

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of certain chickpea genotypes to limit damage from H. armigera

entities.name: WASHED LEAVES
entities.description: Chickpea leaves where surface exudates have been removed by washing

entities.name: UNWASHED LEAVES
entities.description: Chickpea leaves with natural surface exudates intact

entities.name: CHICKPEA GENOTYPES
entities.description: Specific lines of chickpea such as ICC 12475, ICC 12478, ICC 12479, ICC 14876, ICC 12495 and ICC 12494 tested for resistance

entities.name: WATER-SOLUBLE COMPOUNDS
entities.description: Chemical substances in leaf exudates that contribute to pest resistance

entities.name: LEAF EXUDATES
entities.description: Secretions on the surface of chickpea leaves

entities.name: MALIC ACID
entities.description: A specific water-soluble compound found in leaf exudates

entities.name: OXALIC ACID
entities.description: A specific water-soluble compound found in leaf exudates
